name: Zigzag Alternating Split
description: A multi-row alternating image and text layout where each row swaps the image side, ideal for process steps, feature tours, and brand narratives that benefit from visual rhythm.

descriptions.descriptive: Multiple rows of paired image and text where each row alternates the image side, creating a clear left-right reading rhythm. Generous vertical spacing separates rows, and each row supports an optional eyebrow, bullet list, and per-row CTA. Image corners are softly rounded, typography hierarchy is restrained, and the overall composition reads as a narrated sequence rather than a feature dump.
descriptions.usage: Use when you have 2 to 5 related stages, features, or chapters that share a structure but benefit from visual variety. Ideal for process explanations, end-to-end feature tours, founder stories told in beats, or service offerings where each item deserves its own image. Avoid when items have no narrative order or when a denser grid would communicate the same information faster.
descriptions.audienceFit: Fits B2B service firms, agencies, and product teams whose buying audience expects to read carefully before deciding. Resonates with consultancies, design studios, manufacturers explaining their craft, and SaaS companies whose differentiator is process. Less suited to ecommerce or quick-conversion landing pages where dense feature grids outperform sequential reading.
